quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 8 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Ratios of Side Lengths in a Scalene Right Triangle
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, which side is the shortest?
options: The side opposite the right angle
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the shortest side is opposite the smallest angle. In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the smallest angle is 30°, so the side opposite it is the shortest leg.

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, if the shortest leg (opposite the 30° angle) has length $x$, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the hypotenuse is always twice the shortest leg. If the shortest leg is $x$, the hypotenuse is $2x$.

questions.question: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, if the shortest leg (opposite the 30° angle) has length $x$, what is the length of the longer leg (opposite the 60° angle)?
questions.explanation: In a 30°-60°-90° triangle, the longer leg is $\sqrt{3}$ times the shortest leg. If the shortest leg is $x$, the longer leg is $x\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the ratio of the sides in a 30°-60°-90° triangle, from shortest to longest?
questions.explanation: The side ratio in a 30°-60°-90° triangle is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The shortest leg (opposite 30°) is $1$, the longer leg (opposite 60°) is $\sqrt{3}$, and the hypotenuse is $2$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a shortest leg of length $6$. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is twice the shortest leg: $2 \cdot 6 = 12$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a shortest leg of length $4$. What is the length of the longer leg?
questions.explanation: The longer leg (opposite 60°) is the shortest leg times $\sqrt{3}$: $4\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a hypotenuse of length $10$. What is the length of the shortest leg?
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is twice the shortest leg: $2x = 10$, so $x = 5$. The shortest leg is $5$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a hypotenuse of length $14$. What is the length of the longer leg?
questions.explanation: First find the shortest leg: $2x = 14$, so $x = 7$. Then the longer leg is $7\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a longer leg (opposite the 60° angle) of length $5\sqrt{3}$. What is the length of the shortest leg?
questions.explanation: The longer leg equals the shortest leg times $\sqrt{3}$: $x\sqrt{3} = 5\sqrt{3}$, so $x = 5$. The shortest leg is $5$.

questions.question: A 30°-60°-90° triangle has a longer leg (opposite the 60° angle) of length $9\sqrt{3}$. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: The longer leg is $x\sqrt{3} = 9\sqrt{3}$, so the shortest leg $x = 9$. The hypotenuse is twice the shortest leg: $2 \cdot 9 = 18$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the 30°-60°-90° triangle side relationships. You can find any side given any other side with confidence.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember the ratio $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The shortest leg is opposite 30°, the longer leg is opposite 60°, and the hypotenuse is opposite the right angle. The hypotenuse is always twice the shortest leg.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Key patterns: (1) shortest leg $= x$ (opposite 30°), (2) longer leg $= x\sqrt{3}$ (opposite 60°), (3) hypotenuse $= 2x$. Always find the shortest leg first if you're given the longer leg or hypotenuse.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The 30°-60°-90° triangle comes from splitting an equilateral triangle in half along its altitude. The side ratio is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$. The hypotenuse is always twice the shortest leg.
